_note: Authored debate spines for the Timeline rail. The graph links cannot define a spine's order on their own (builds edges run both directions in time, hinges are unlinked), so a human asserts which ideas, in what order, and how each move relates to the one before. Node `id` must match a concept id in ideas.json; `era` is read from there at runtime, so spines MUST be authored in ascending era (the rail draws true time, left to right). `rel` is the move THIS node makes against the previous one: opens / builds (real inheritance) / rejects / echoes (an independent rhyme between cultures that never met) / reframes. `shape` is documentation only (the renderer ignores it). `status` drives the margin sub-label and the single gold terminus seal, which renders ONLY for live-rivals. One debate is the flagship (opens the tab); the rest sit in the margin index.

nodes.caption: I could have chosen otherwise — the will is genuinely undetermined.
Free and determined at once — a free act is simply an uncoerced one.
Every choice has prior causes; ‘free will’ is the illusion.
There is no escaping choice — we are sentenced to freedom.
The innermost self just is the absolute — one unchanging soul.
Look closely and no self is found — only changing processes.
The self is only a bundle of perceptions; no owner sits behind them.
The self is a model the brain builds — a useful illusion.
Real knowledge is grasping the eternal Forms behind the shadows we take for the world.
We can be certain of nothing — so the wise suspend judgment.
One thing survives every doubt: that I am doubting. I think, therefore I am.
Nothing reaches the mind except through the senses; knowledge is built from experience, not pure reason.
Knowledge isn’t what we’ve proven true, but what has survived every honest attempt to prove it false.
All motion traces back to a first cause that moves everything without itself being moved.
The ultimate is real but empty of any creator-God — an unconditioned ground, not a ruler above the world.
Beyond being, beyond even thought, lies the One — the ineffable source from which all things overflow.
There is no god but God. Islamic philosophy read the Neoplatonists and refounded that One as the single divine unity.
God and Nature are one and the same; the Absolute is not beyond the world, it is the world.
We have killed God; there is no Absolute left to anchor our values.
Life is a wheel of death and rebirth; the aim is release — moksha — from the whole turning.
Suffering has a cause — craving — and an end: the eightfold path that quiets it.
Suffering comes not from events but from our judgments of them; master the judgment and be unshaken.
Schopenhauer, reading the Upanishads, agreed: suffering is the will’s endless craving — so quiet the will.
There is no cure and no escape; we revolt and live without appeal. Imagine Sisyphus happy.
